standard_variable.semantic_grain: one row per anchor-qualified summary target

database_mapping.source_code_system: Amsterdam numericitems itemid via upstream std_icu_urine_output_event

identifier_normalization.hadm_id_role: not separately available in the current Amsterdam public mapping and retained as nullable context
identifier_normalization.stay_id_role: raw admissionid is the Amsterdam ICU/MC local admission identifier and normalizes to stay_id for ICU-semantic same-name outputs

database_mapping.source_grain: one row per approved Amsterdam ICU urine-output event from std_icu_urine_output_event before first-day stay-level reduction
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained row per Amsterdam ICU-semantic stay-equivalent admission

target_scope.scope_note: MC-only Amsterdam admissions are intentionally excluded from this same-name ICU first-day urine-output summary.

summary_build_translation.window_start_translation: start at admissions_core.admittedat for ICU-semantic Amsterdam admissions
summary_build_translation.window_end_translation: end at admissions_core.admittedat + 24 hours for the aggregation numerator
summary_build_translation.qualifying_row_translation: keep approved upstream std_icu_urine_output_event rows where event_time_offset_ms_raw >= admittedat and event_time_offset_ms_raw <= admittedat + 86400000 ms
summary_build_translation.selection_translation: include all qualifying first-day urine-output event rows for each normalized stay_id
summary_build_translation.aggregation_translation: sum urine_output_ml per stay_id as first_day_urine_output_ml and retain raw-sum audit context from urine_output_ml_raw
summary_build_translation.tie_break_translation: not applicable because all qualifying rows are summed
summary_build_translation.no_source_row_translation: retain every ICU-semantic target stay and set first_day_urine_output_ml to NULL when no qualifying first-day approved urine value exists
summary_build_translation.partial_window_translation: retain observed-window context and partial-window caution flags, but do not clip the first-day aggregation numerator by dischargedat

representation_and_normalization.source_unit_expectation: upstream std_icu_urine_output_event has already normalized retained Amsterdam source rows to mL
representation_and_normalization.output_rounding_rule: retain current float precision from upstream approved event values

validation_contract.no_source_row_policy_check: first_day_urine_output_ml may be NULL only when has_any_approved_first_day_urine_value_flag is false and source_approved_value_row_count is zero
notes: Amsterdam retained absolute time is a raw milliseconds offset and should not be interpreted as a wall-clock datetime.
The Amsterdam denominator is ICU-semantic admissions only: IC, IC&MC, and MC&IC.
MC-only admissions remain outside this same-name ICU summary and require a separate variable or explicit scope decision if needed.
Rows without approved first-day urine values are retained as NULL, not converted to zero.
The aggregation uses the approved upstream urine-output event asset and should not rescan raw numericitems for this summary.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: The governed execution.py entrypoint reads this mapping spec and delegates to the current local reviewed implementation until a more fully spec-native runner replaces it.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal std_first_day_urine_output_summary outputs for AmsterdamUMCdb-1.0.2 should be produced only through the governed execution.py path rather than side scripts or ad hoc rebuilds.
